Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Search emails for Dr. Mehta or Canopy Health relocation communications using search_emails (queries: "Mehta", "Canopy", "UrbanNest", "housing") to find the housing options email Fatimah sent, Carmen Reyes's response with three options (River North $1,250/wk, Streeterville $1,350/wk, Gold Coast $1,450/wk), and determine whether Dr. Mehta has replied with his preference. Expected finding: Dr. Mehta has NOT responded to the housing options email.
2. Search emails or Slack for Carmen Reyes / UrbanNest availability hold status using search_emails (query: "Carmen", "UrbanNest", "hold") and/or conversations_search_messages (query: "hold", "UrbanNest", "Mehta housing"). Expected finding: the 48 hour holds on all three UrbanNest options expire end of day April 25, discovered via Linear comment on ticket #343 or Fatimah's consolidated status update in #operations Slack.
3. Search emails or Slack for Swift Relocations confirmation on the equipment transport and household move using search_emails (query: "Swift", "Greg Pallone", "Mehta") and/or conversations_search_messages (query: "Swift", "Mehta", "Boston pickup"). Expected finding: Swift Relocations has NOT been engaged yet for Dr. Mehta's move. Per the consolidated Slack status update in #operations, Workstream 3 (Household Move) is "Pending, will engage Swift once housing is confirmed." The housing selection (and thus the Chicago delivery address) must be locked in before Swift can be formally booked. There is no outstanding request to Greg Pallone for the Mehta relocation at this point, and no household move quote has been requested yet.
4. Search Slack #operations or #finance for Marcus Thorne's estimate status using conversations_search_messages (query: "estimate", "Mehta") or search QuickBooks using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria related to Canopy/Mehta). Expected finding: Marcus has already completed estimate EST 2026 0422 for $12,170 in QuickBooks, flagged as priority billing with Net 15 terms. The estimate is ready.
5. Search CRM for the Canopy Health deal and engagement history using crm_search_deals (dealname: "Mehta") or crm_search_companies (name: "Canopy Health") to find the deal record and account context. Expected finding: deal "Canopy Health — Dr. Anand Mehta Executive Relocation (Boston to Chicago, May 2026)" with amount $12,170, stage "inprogress", close date May 7 2026 (matching estimate EST 2026 0422). CRM engagement note documents this as Canopy Health's first C-suite placement with MoveOps, flagging strategic significance — Canopy is MoveOps's longest-standing client (since July 2024). The deal description includes the full 7-line-item service package breakdown confirming the financial picture is buttoned up.
6. Log a CRM engagement on the Canopy Health deal using crm_create_engagement with a note summarizing the current relocation status findings: housing pending Dr. Mehta's selection with holds expiring, equipment chain-of-custody documentation still outstanding, household move not yet engaged with Swift, flights not booked, and estimate confirmed at $12,170. This documents the status check and any discrepancies discovered between the deal record and actual progress.
7. Search emails or Slack for flight booking status using search_emails (query: "flight", "Mehta", "Atlas") and/or conversations_search_messages (query: "flight", "Mehta"). Expected finding: flights have not been booked yet. The estimate includes $370 for flight booking with premium cabin upgrade (2 passengers, partner comfort) but no Atlas Corporate Travel booking confirmation exists.
8. Search Linear for Dr. Mehta tracking tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "Mehta" or project: "proj_canopy") to find all three workstream tickets. Expected finding: three tickets exist. Ticket #343 "Temporary Housing, Dr. Anand Mehta, Chicago River North" (due May 1), ticket #375 "Equipment Packing and Chain of Custody, Dr. Mehta, Medical Research Equipment" (due April 30), and ticket #396 "Household Move, Dr. Anand Mehta, Boston to Chicago" (due May 3). All assigned to Fatimah, all in state "In Progress."
9. Read Linear comments on the housing ticket using linear_list_comments (issueId for ticket #343) to discover the UrbanNest options details and the 48 hour hold deadline. Expected finding: comment by Fatimah noting Option B (Streeterville) is recommended, and Dr. Mehta has until end of day April 25 before holds expire.
10. Search Airtable for Dr. Mehta's relocation record using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records (base/table for Relocations, searching for "Mehta" or "Anand"). Expected finding: no Airtable relocation record exists for Dr. Mehta. The agent must create one.
11. Look up contact details for Dr. Mehta, Carmen Reyes (UrbanNest), Greg Pallone (Swift), and Patricia Langford (Canopy Health) using contacts_search_contacts (queries: "Mehta", "Carmen", "Greg Pallone", "Patricia Langford") to obtain email addresses for outreach. Expected: anand.mehta@canopyhealth.io, carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com, greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com, patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io.
12. Send a follow up email to Dr. Mehta (via send_email) at anand.mehta@canopyhealth.io from fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com requesting his housing preference, noting that the 48 hour UrbanNest holds are expiring and a decision is needed urgently.
13. Send an email to Greg Pallone at Swift Relocations (via send_email) at greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com from fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com reaching out to confirm capacity and request a quote for the April 28 to 30 Boston pack and load of Dr. Mehta's equipment (Eizo monitors, secured filing cabinet) and the long distance household move to Chicago.
14. Send an email to Patricia Langford at Canopy Health (via send_email) at patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io from fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com requesting the chain of custody documentation template and the compliance team contact for Dr. Mehta's secured filing cabinet handoff. The Slack consolidated status update and Linear comment on ticket #375 both identify this as the current blocker for the equipment workstream.
15. Update Linear ticket #343 (Temporary Housing) using linear_update_issue or linear_create_comment with current status: UrbanNest options presented, Option B (Streeterville) recommended, Dr. Mehta has not responded, 48 hour holds expiring April 25, follow up email sent.
16. Update Linear ticket #375 (Equipment Packing and Chain of Custody) using linear_update_issue or linear_create_comment with current status: equipment inventory confirmed (2x Eizo RadiForce RX660 monitors, SentrySafe DH 109E filing cabinet), chain of custody documentation requirements defined, current blocker is obtaining the chain of custody documentation template from Canopy Health's compliance team (Fatimah has not been told who on their compliance side to contact, needs to email Patricia Langford to get that contact and the template), follow up email sent to Patricia.
17. Update Linear ticket #396 (Household Move) using linear_update_issue or linear_create_comment with current status: move planned via Swift Relocations but Swift has not yet been contacted for a quote. Engagement is planned once housing selection is confirmed and the delivery address is known, flights not yet booked ($370 budgeted for 2 passengers via Atlas Corporate Travel), blocker is housing confirmation.
18. Create or update the Airtable relocation record for Dr. Mehta using airtable_create_record (on the Relocations table) with fields: Name = "Dr. Anand Mehta", Company = "Canopy Health", Status = "In Progress", Origin = "Boston, MA", Destination = "Chicago, IL", Move Start Date = "2026 04 28" (proposed pack date per Slack timeline; Dr. Mehta flexible on April 28-30), Assigned Coordinator = "Fatimah Al Rashidi", Special Requirements noting the Eizo monitors, secured filing cabinet with chain of custody, temporary housing pending, and white glove executive service.
19. Discover who manages the Canopy Health account on the client engagement side using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records on the Client Accounts table (searching for "Canopy Health") to find the Account Manager field, or by searching CRM engagements and deal descriptions for Canopy Health (via crm_search_deals or crm_list_engagements) where Catalina's name appears in account context notes. Expected finding: Catalina Dubois is the Account Manager responsible for Canopy Health. Look up her contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Catalina Dubois"). Expected: catalina.dubois@moveops.com.
20 .Send an email or Slack message to Catalina Dubois (via send_email at catalina.dubois@moveops.com from fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com, or via conversations_add_message as a DM) briefing her on the current state of the Dr. Mehta executive relocation so she is not blindsided if Canopy Health reaches out. The message should cover the key status points: housing pending Dr. Mehta's decision with holds expiring, equipment documentation still outstanding with Canopy's compliance team, household move not yet engaged, and flights not yet booked.
21. Post the full consolidated status update in #operations Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id for #operations) summarizing all three workstreams: (1) Housing, options sent, awaiting Dr. Mehta's preference, holds expiring, follow up sent; (2) Equipment, inventory confirmed, chain of custody defined, Swift confirmation pending, follow up sent; (3) Household Move, quote pending from Swift, flights not booked; plus Marcus's estimate is ready ($12,170). Posted so Chloe Vance has full visibility.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata
